summary:
Anti-scatter grids increase patient radiation exposure because they absorb primary x-rays, necessitating higher mAs to maintain image density. Although this does not involve prolongation of exposure time per se, the overall radiation dose rises significantly, making increased patient exposure—a recognized drawback—accurately described under Option A.  

{{A}}
formatted_summary:

Although this does not involve prolongation of exposure time per se, the overall radiation dose rises significantly, making increased patient exposure—a recognized drawback—accurately described under Option A.  

{{A}}. Answer choice: A
